$comment: CURRENT-2424 pilot eval set (Categories + Collections subset of the eventual fixed 25-prompt set). Every `expected` was verified against api-reference/storefront-v2026-04.yaml. Path templates use the same {placeholder} names as the spec; the grader treats any {placeholder} or concrete value in a placeholder segment as equivalent. auth: 'none' = spec `security: []` (public storefront surface), 'bearer' = spec `bearer_auth` (company surface).

prompts.prompt: I'm building an unauthenticated storefront browse page. What API call lists the categories that are live on a company's public storefront? I just need the public catalog for the current shop, no login.
prompts.notes: Public Categories catalog. Spec line 3492-3504: storefrontCategoriesIndex, security: []. Company resolved from subdomain, so no auth.

prompts.prompt: I have a collection's URL slug ("wellness-essentials") and need to render that single collection on a public, no-login storefront page. What's the request to fetch it by slug?
prompts.notes: Public Collection by slug. Spec line 6609-6620: storefrontCollectionShow, security: []. Resolves by slug path param.

prompts.prompt: As a company admin with an API bearer token, how do I create a brand-new category titled "Summer Sale"?
prompts.notes: Create a Category. Spec line 3711-3731: companyCategoriesCreate, bearer_auth. Body wrapped in `category`; required field is `title` (CategoryCreateRequest line 590-598).

prompts.prompt: Using our company admin token, I need to rename an existing collection (id 5102) to "Holiday Picks". What's the exact request?
prompts.notes: Update a Collection. Spec line 6800-6822: companyCollectionsUpdate uses PATCH, bearer_auth. Body wrapped in `collection`; rename sets `title` (CollectionWrite line 2971).

prompts.prompt: On the public (no-auth) storefront, fetch the first 50 top-level categories that are available in the United Kingdom, returned in French, sorted alphabetically by title (A to Z). What's the full request with query parameters?
prompts.notes: Public Categories catalog with filters. filter[country]=GB (line 118-119), filter[language]=fr (line 130-131), filter[parent_id]=root for top-level (line 3518-3521), sort=title (line 166-175), page[limit]=50 (line 107-108). All names verified verbatim.

prompts.prompt: How do I permanently delete the category with id 4821 as a company admin (I have a bearer token)?
prompts.notes: Destroy a Category. Spec line 3888-3902: companyCategoriesDestroy, DELETE, bearer_auth, returns 204.

prompts.prompt: I want the category with id 4821 to automatically go live at 2026-08-01T08:00:00Z. As a company admin, what single call schedules it to publish at that time?
prompts.notes: Scheduling is done via update (there is no separate publish endpoint). Spec line 3847-3871: companyCategoriesUpdate PATCH, bearer. Set status=scheduled + publish_at (CategoryWrite line 533-538). Body wrapped in `category`.

prompts.prompt: We're only selling a collection (id 5102) in Great Britain and the United States. As a company admin, how do I restrict that collection's country availability to just GB and US?
prompts.notes: Country availability is a write field, not a query param, on the company surface. Spec CollectionWrite line 2999-3005: country_isos array restricts availability. Set via companyCollectionsUpdate PATCH (line 6800). Body wrapped in `collection`.

prompts.prompt: On the public storefront, how do I fetch only the child categories nested directly under the parent category whose id is 4821?
prompts.notes: Hierarchy / children-of filter. Spec line 3518-3524: filter[parent_id] takes a category id (or 'root' for top-level). Only exists on the public categories index, not the company index. Value would be 4821.

prompts.prompt: As a company admin, how do I pull the aggregate share statistics for the category with id 4821?
prompts.notes: Share statistics for a Category. Spec line 3913-3922: companyCategoriesShares, GET, bearer_auth, read-only aggregate.

prompts.prompt: I'm building an email login screen. When a user types their email address, what API call sends them a one-time login passcode by email? No user is signed in yet.
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_send_mfa. `email` is a required query param; `fluid_shop` is optional; security: [] (no auth). Auth paths are unversioned /api/... (not v202604).

prompts.prompt: After we emailed the passcode, the user entered their code. I have the MFA uuid returned from the send step. What request confirms the code and returns the JWTs for the user's companies?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_confirm_mfa. Request body requires `uuid` plus `multi_factor_authentication.verification_code`; security: [] (no auth). Grader flattens nested body keys, so the leaf `verification_code` matches.

prompts.prompt: On our public sign-in page I want to show a company's name and logo before anyone logs in. Given the shop name "acme-wellness", what unauthenticated call returns that company's basic public details?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_company_details. `fluid_shop` is a path param; security: [] (public). Returns CompanyPublicDetails.

prompts.prompt: My server-side integration already holds a user-company token and needs a regular user JWT to call authenticated endpoints (this is not a customer session). What request exchanges that user-company token for a JWT?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_token_exchange. Body requires `user_company_token`; security: [] because the token travels in the body, not the Authorization header. Disambiguated from customer_token_exchange (customer JWT) and jwt_exchange (reverse direction).

prompts.prompt: Our session JWT is about to expire. What single call refreshes an existing JWT to extend its validity?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_jwt_refresh. Body requires `jwt`; security: [] (the JWT to refresh is supplied in the body).

prompts.prompt: I want to render the available social login buttons (Google, Apple, Facebook) for the shop "acme-wellness" on the login page. What call lists the social auth providers and their OAuth URLs for that shop?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_social_auth_providers. `fluid_shop` is a required query param; security: [] (public).

prompts.prompt: A signed-in user belongs to several companies and picked company 512 to switch into. Using their bearer token, what call switches the authenticated user to that company and returns a fresh JWT?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_switch_company. `company_id` is a path param; security: bearer_auth. Distinct from GET /api/authentication/company/{fluid_shop} (public branding).

prompts.prompt: As a Fluid root admin with a bearer token, how do I start impersonating the company with id 42?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/auth-v0.yaml, operationId auth_v0_impersonate_company. Body requires `company_id`; security: bearer_auth (root admins only). Returns 201 with the impersonation JWT.

prompts.prompt: I'm building the very first step of checkout — no customer is logged in yet. For shop "acme-wellness", what request creates a brand-new cart for a US-based shopper?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_create_cart (spec line 8561-8710). Required body fields: fluid_shop, country_code (line 8707-8709). security: [] — cart creation is unauthenticated.

prompts.prompt: A shopper picked a size and color variant on the product page and I already have their cart token. What request adds that variant and quantity to the existing cart?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_add_cart_items (spec line 9236-9364). Top-level required body field is `items` (line 9360-9362), an array of {variant_id, quantity} entries. security: bearer_auth.

prompts.prompt: A shopper typed the promo code SAVE10 into the cart's discount field. Using the cart token, what request applies that code to the cart?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_apply_cart_discount (spec line 8900-8950). Required body field: discount_code (line 8946-8948). security: bearer_auth. Not idempotent — reapplying the same code errors.

prompts.prompt: Before showing the email-confirmation step at checkout, I want to silently check whether the email a shopper just typed belongs to an existing customer — without creating a new account if it doesn't. What request performs that returning-buyer probe on the cart?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_send_cart_magic_link (spec line 8745-8809). Required body fields: email, fluid_shop (line 8797-8799). The probe behavior is the optional `lookup_only: true` flag — a 404 means no existing customer, telling the frontend to treat the email as a guest and skip the confirmation step. security: bearer_auth.

prompts.prompt: A shopper on cart cart_9f2 wants to convert one of the cart's variants into a recurring subscription, billing to their saved address going forward. What request creates that subscription?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_create_subscription (spec line 11977-12066, distinct from the GET list at line 11771). Body wrapped in `subscription`; required nested fields: subscription_plan_id, customer_id, variant_id, address_id, cart_token, timezone, next_bill_date (line 12026-12033). security: bearer_auth.

prompts.prompt: A customer on an active subscription (token sb_5567) wants to bump their recurring item quantity from 1 to 2. Using the subscription token, what request updates just the quantity?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_update_subscription (spec line 12105-12185). Body must be wrapped in a `subscription` object (required, line 12146-12147, minProperties 1); `quantity` is one of its writable properties (line 12175-12176). security: bearer_auth.

prompts.prompt: A customer just finished checkout and landed on the order-confirmation page carrying an order token in the URL. What unauthenticated request fetches that order to render the confirmation?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_get_order (spec line 11282-11317). security: [] — explicitly documented as a public endpoint, no authentication required.

prompts.prompt: A prospect clicked an enrollment link carrying a token and needs to see the enrollment form before filling it out. What public request retrieves that enrollment by token?
prompts.notes: Verified against api-reference/checkout-v2026-04.yaml, operationId checkout_v2026_04_get_enrollment (spec line 11102-11129). security: [] — public endpoint per spec description.
